hard_disqualifiers: LATE PROPOSALS WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED. Proposals delivered after the said deadline will not be accepted.
Failure to complete and submit the required documents and attachments may result in your proposal being deemed non-responsive.
Failure to complete and submit the DBE forms according to the above instructions will render your bid non-responsive.
Fax submission will not be acceptable.
This form [Cover Sheet] must be completed in its entirety. Deviations and failure to complete and sign this form will deem your proposal non-responsive.

requirements.text: Technical Approach and Staffing Capability (25%). Proposer's demonstrated ability to recruit, retain, and manage qualified IT professionals across all service categories in Section 2.2, including depth of candidate pipeline, workforce management processes, and experience supporting technical environments. Proposals demonstrating capability in Cybersecurity, OT, or Information Management receive favorable consideration.
requirements.what_it_measures: Depth of IT talent pipeline, workforce management maturity, breadth across IT Operations and Specialized Technical Services; bonus credit for Cybersecurity/OT/IM capability.

requirements.text: Relevant Experience and Past Performance (25%). Proposer's record of providing IT professional services to public-sector or utility clients of comparable size and complexity; quality of client references; demonstrated continuity of service delivery.
requirements.what_it_measures: Track record with public-sector / utility clients of similar scale; reference quality; service continuity evidence.

requirements.text: Management Approach and Transition Plan (15%). Proposer's contract management structure; quality assurance processes; reporting capabilities; knowledge transfer methodology; plan for onboarding and transitioning personnel at contract start.
requirements.what_it_measures: Contract Manager qualifications, QA discipline, reporting systems, 90-day transition plan, knowledge transfer approach, incumbent staff retention strategy.

requirements.text: Cost (15%). Cost score computed as CS = (LPC/PC * 15) where LPC = lowest proposed cost of all proposers and PC = proposer's cost. Evaluated on total estimated annual cost for base year staffing complement at fully burdened hourly rates.
requirements.what_it_measures: Fully burdened hourly rates across all position categories in the standardized staffing scenario (2,080 hours/year each). Lowest cost receives full 15 points.

requirements.text: Organizational Capacity and Financial Stability (10%). Company background; financial statements or D&B report; organizational depth; insurance capacity; completeness of proposal response.
requirements.what_it_measures: Financial health (2 years audited statements or D&B), org depth to absorb a 55+ person engagement, insurance capacity meeting minimums, completeness of submission.

requirements.text: DBE Participation (10%). Proposer's plan to meet or exceed the DBE participation goal of 25% established for this solicitation. DBE firms must be SLDBE or LaUCP certified.
requirements.what_it_measures: Credibility and specificity of plan to achieve 25% DBE contract spend using SLDBE/LaUCP-certified firms.

requirements.text: Proposals must be received by [Agency] Procurement Department by June 11, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. CDT local time. LATE PROPOSALS WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.

Electronic Submission: (1) Digitally signed technical proposal and (1) digitally signed cost proposal in searchable PDF format to [email redacted]. Subject Line: 2026-SWB-30 – Information Technology Professional Services – [Proposer Name]. Fax submission will not be acceptable.
Cost proposals must be submitted in a separate envelope or file (if emailed) marked 'Cost Proposal'. Cost and technical proposals must be submitted as separate files.

From the time of advertising, and until the final award, there is a prohibition on communication by Respondents (or anyone on their behalf) with Board's staff, Selection Committee members and elected officials. Breaking the established prohibition on communication may result in a disqualification of the proposal.

ATTACHMENT – COVER SHEET: Must be completed in its entirety and digitally signed by an authorized representative. Deviations and failure to complete and sign this form will deem your proposal non-responsive.

ATTACHMENT – COMPANY BACKGROUND FORM: Required for proposal submission. Must include company legal name, headquarters address, nearest office to [City], year established, states of incorporation, [State] business license status, total employees, IT professionals employed, DUNS number, Federal Tax ID.

ATTACHMENT – QUESTIONS: Seven mandatory questions covering IT staffing history, public-sector contracts, geographic recruitment reach, strategic alliances, employee retention rate, outstanding litigation, and subcontractor intent. Must be completed as part of Proposal Section 2.

ATTACHMENT – STAFFING PLAN FORM: Required for proposal submission. Must show each position category with minimum education, minimum years experience, required certifications, average time to fill (days), and proposed hourly rate range. Must also include org chart, Contract Manager resume, emergency staffing plan (ER1/ER2).

ATTACHMENT – CLIENT REFERENCE FORM: Must provide at least five (5) references from clients for whom Proposer has provided IT professional services of comparable scope and complexity within the past five (5) years. At least three (3) references must be from public-sector or utility clients.

EDBP Participation Summary Sheet Form (separate file): Name, address and phone of each DBE firm; scope of work; dollar value for each DBE; signature of prime on form. Required at proposal submission. Failure to submit renders bid non-responsive.
EDBP Acknowledgement of Negotiated Terms Form (separate file, one per DBE firm): Signed by both prime and DBE subcontractor. DBE must be SLDBE or LaUCP certified. Required at proposal submission.

ATTACHMENT – PRICING FORM (Excel format, separate file): Fully burdened hourly rates for each position category for base year and four option years. Emergency pay rates as separate line items. Time-and-materials primary structure. Contract Manager not a separately billable position.

2.2.1 IT Operations (Core Scope): Contractor shall provide personnel for Help Desk and End-User Support, PC Deployment and Lifecycle Management, Enterprise Application Support (Oracle Fusion ERP, CIS/Billing, EAMS/Trimble Unity, Dayforce, GIS), Database Administration (Oracle, SQL Server), GIS Operations (ESRI ArcGIS Enterprise), Network Administration and Infrastructure Operations (LAN/WAN/VPN/firewall), Server and Data Center Operations, Web Administration and Digital Content, Audio-Visual and Meeting Support, Baseline Cybersecurity Operations, and Emergency and Continuity Operations.
requirements.what_it_measures: Coverage of all IT Operations functional areas listed in Section 2.2.1

requirements.text: 2.2.2 Specialized Technical Services: Contractor shall provide Project Managers for enterprise modernization, Cloud Engineers for Azure and other cloud environments, Database Architects and Senior Administrators, Network Engineers for security architecture, and Enterprise Application Specialists for Oracle Fusion, Trimble Unity, Dayforce, and GIS Utility Network.
requirements.what_it_measures: Ability to place specialized senior roles; Oracle, Azure, GIS Utility Network experience

requirements.text: 2.2.3 Cybersecurity Services (at Board discretion): Services may include GRC support, Identity and Access Management (IAM) administration, Security Operations Center (SOC) operations or SOC-as-a-Service, vulnerability management and assessment, incident response support, and security awareness program support. Capability not required for a responsive proposal but will receive favorable consideration.
requirements.what_it_measures: Favorable consideration for demonstrated cybersecurity staffing capability (GRC, IAM, SOC)

requirements.text: 2.2.4 Operational Technology / SCADA: The Proposer shall include a minimum of 40 hours of monthly SCADA application support in their base bid. This support covers PI Data Archive, PI Asset Framework, PI Vision, and iFIX HMI/SCADA systems. The Proposer may fulfill this requirement through a qualified subcontractor.
requirements.what_it_measures: Ability to provide or subcontract SCADA support (PI Suite, iFIX); minimum 40 hours/month must be priced in base bid

requirements.text: 2.2.5 Information Management (at Board discretion): Services may include administrative workflow modernization, digitization, process automation (Digital Transformation branch) and records retention, compliance documentation, scanning and digitization (Records Management branch) aligned with ISO 15489 and NARA standards.
requirements.what_it_measures: Favorable consideration for IM capability; ISO 15489 and NARA records management knowledge

requirements.text: 2.2.11.5 Key Personnel: Contractor shall designate a Contract Manager ([State]-based, not separately billable, included in management overhead) and Technical Leads for each major functional area. Contract Manager requires prior written [Agency] IT Director approval to replace; 30 calendar days advance notice for planned changes.
requirements.what_it_measures: [State]-based Contract Manager identified with resume; Technical Leads named per functional area

requirements.text: 2.2.11.6 Subcontractors: Contractor may use subcontractors subject to prior written Board approval. Proposer shall identify proposed subcontractors and their roles in the proposal submission.

2.2.11.7 Personnel Retention: Contractor shall maintain an annual retention rate of not less than 85% for personnel assigned to this contract, calculated over 12 consecutive months. Contractor's proposal shall describe its approach to retaining personnel.
requirements.what_it_measures: Commitment to 85% retention rate; narrative on compensation, career development, and retention practices

requirements.text: 2.2.13 Emergency Operations: Contractor shall designate Tier 1 (ER1) personnel for on-site continuous IT support during activated emergency operations (up to 12 hours/day, including curfew conditions) and Tier 2 (ER2) personnel available to return on short notice. Emergency staffing rosters must be established before each hurricane season (June 1) and updated as changes occur.
requirements.what_it_measures: Hurricane/emergency staffing plan credibility; ER1/ER2 designation process; business continuity posture

requirements.text: 2.2.14 Background Checks: All personnel requiring access to [Agency] systems or facilities shall complete background checks including criminal history (7 years), employment verification (5 years), education verification, professional license verification, and sex offender registry search.

2.2.15 Cybersecurity Incident Notification: Contractor shall notify [Agency] within two (2) hours of discovery of any cybersecurity incident that may affect [Agency] data or systems. Contractor shall implement reasonable administrative, technical, and physical safeguards consistent with applicable cybersecurity standards.

2.2.6(e) Reporting Requirements: Contractor shall provide weekly Project Status Reports, monthly Help Desk Metrics, monthly System Availability Reports, monthly Position Status Reports, monthly Security Incident Summaries, quarterly Work Plan Updates, quarterly DBE Utilization Reports, annual Strategic Recommendations, annual Disaster Recovery Test Results, and annual Policy and Procedure Reviews.
requirements.what_it_measures: Reporting infrastructure and cadence; proposer must demonstrate systems for weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting

requirements.text: 2.9 Insurance Requirements: Contractor shall maintain Workers' Compensation (statutory + $1M Employers Liability), Commercial General Liability ($1M each occurrence / $2M aggregate), Business Automobile ($1M combined single limit), and Errors & Omissions / Professional Liability ($1M each claim / $2M annual aggregate, 5-year tail). Certificates must be filed with [Agency] Risk Manager before work commences.
requirements.what_it_measures: Evidence of adequate insurance capacity at required minimums

requirements.text: 4.4 Company Background and Qualifications: Must provide audited financial information for past two (2) completed fiscal years (income statements, balance sheets, statements of cash flows). Privately held companies must provide a current Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) report.
requirements.what_it_measures: Financial stability; audited financials or D&B report required

requirements.text: 4.5 Technical Approach and Staffing Capability: Proposer must address recruitment and retention methodology, workforce management approach, demonstrated capability to support IT Operations and Specialized Technical Services (Microsoft 365, SQL Server/Oracle, data center ops, cloud solutions, help desk, IT networking, GIS). Proposers encouraged to also demonstrate Cybersecurity, OT, and IM capability.
requirements.what_it_measures: Breadth of technical capability narrative; Microsoft 365, Oracle, SQL Server, GIS, cloud, help desk all specifically named

requirements.text: 4.6 Management Approach and Transition Plan: Must address transition plan for first 90 days including onboarding incumbent personnel, knowledge transfer from outgoing contractor, emergency and business continuity staffing plan per Section 2.9, and plan for maintaining operations during knowledge transfer period including dual-staffing for first 30-60 days if needed.
requirements.what_it_measures: Credibility of 90-day transition plan; approach to retaining incumbent staff from predecessor contract expiring November 2026

requirements.text: 4.7 Staffing Plan and Position Qualifications: Must provide detailed staffing plan with proposed organizational chart, minimum qualifications for each position category (education, certifications, years experience, technical competencies), average time to fill positions by category, and approach to retaining personnel. Must also include resumes for proposed Contract Manager and any identified Technical Leads.
requirements.what_it_measures: Staffing plan completeness; Contract Manager resume required; position qualifications documented

requirements.text: 2.14 Living Wage: Contractor agrees to abide by City Code sections 70-801, et seq. (Living Wage Ordinance). Current living wage is $16.01 per hour. Covered employees must receive at least $16.01/hour and seven days per year of compensated leave. Failure to comply may result in termination.

2.2.12.1 Separation and Fee Restrictions: Contractor shall not impose non-compete clauses, conversion fees, buyout charges, or other contractual barriers that would prevent Contractor personnel from applying for or accepting Civil Service employment with [Agency]. No conversion fee applies after 26 consecutive weeks of service.

2.2.18 Performance Standards and Remedies: Position vacancy beyond 90 days triggers 25% monthly penalty; beyond 180 days triggers 50% penalty. Critical system (Tier 1 or Tier 2) outage response required within 1 hour of notification or $1,000 per occurrence penalty. Status meetings twice monthly or $250 per missed occurrence. Annual corporate visit required or $5,000 penalty.
requirements.what_it_measures: Vendor understanding of and commitment to meeting performance standards and penalty regime

requirements.text: 2.8 Convicted Felon Statement: The Contractor confirms that no principal, member, or officer of the Contractor has, within the preceding 5 years, been convicted of or pled guilty to a felony under state or federal statutes for embezzlement, theft of public funds, bribery, or falsification or destruction of public records.

bench_gaps: RFP requires SCADA application support (PI Data Archive, PI Asset Framework, PI Vision, iFIX HMI) — minimum 40 hours/month in base bid. No cstaff in current bench has documented SCADA/OT platform expertise. Fulfillable via qualified subcontractor per Section 2.2.4.
RFP Section 2.1.6 references NIST SP 800-82 (OT cybersecurity) and IEC 62443 compliance. No cstaff certification in IEC 62443 or NIST 800-82 on bench.
RFP requires Contract Manager to be [State]-based with regular on-site presence at [Agency] facilities. No cstaff profile confirms [State] residency or [City] metro presence.

RFP requires at least 5 client references, 3 of which must be public-sector or utility clients, for IT staffing services within the past 5 years. Bench past-performance YAMLs should be verified to confirm at least 3 public-sector/utility IT staffing engagements are documented.
